title: Caddy Web Server Configuration
description: Configuration variables for deploying and configuring the Caddy Web Server, an automatic HTTPS capable reverse proxy.

CADDY_VERSION.description: Specific version of caddy to install. Can be a numeric version or an alias.

LIBSCRIPT_GLOBAL_INSTALL_METHOD.description: Global override for how software should be installed across all systems (e.g. system package manager vs downloaded binaries/from-source).

LIBSCRIPT_WINDOWS_PKG_MGR.description: Global package manager override for Windows targets (e.g. winget, choco).

CADDY_INSTALL_METHOD.description: How to install CADDY. 'system' uses the native OS package manager, 'source' compiles/downloads binaries.

LIBSCRIPT_LISTEN_SOCKET.description: Global unix socket to listen on

CADDY_LISTEN_PORT.description: Port for CADDY to listen on

CADDY_LISTEN_ADDRESS.description: Address for CADDY to listen on

CADDY_LISTEN_SOCKET.description: Unix socket for CADDY to listen on

WWWROOT_NAME.description: The primary domain or server name (e.g., example.com) to bind the web server to.

WWWROOT_PATH.description: The absolute path to the static site document root (where index.html resides).

WWWROOT_LISTEN.description: The HTTP port Caddy should listen on (defaults to 80, but 443 is used automatically for HTTPS).

WWWROOT_HTTPS_PROVIDER.description: The HTTPS provider for automatic certificates. Usually 'letsencrypt' or 'zerossl' (Caddy uses both by default if not set).

CADDY_SERVICE_RUN_AS_USER.description: Windows local user account to run the service under (leave empty for Network Service).

CADDY_SERVICE_RUN_AS_PASSWORD.description: Password for the Windows local user account (if applicable).

CADDY_SERVICE_NAME.description: Custom name for the Windows Service (allows side-by-side installations).
